adds.rationale: Robotics is named in the profile as a next wave and Q1-2024 has the first commercial-grade catalysts: Figure AI's $675M raise from MSFT/NVDA/Bezos/OpenAI (Feb 29) and NVIDIA GTC's Project GR00T/Isaac humanoid platform (Mar 18) put the buildup phase on the clock.

news_evidence.summary: Figure AI raises $675M Series B at $2.6B valuation from Microsoft, Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, OpenAI, and Intel Capital to build general-purpose humanoid robots; OpenAI collaboration announced same week.
Nvidia unveils Project GR00T humanoid-robot foundation model and Jetson Thor compute platform at GTC 2024 keynote, framing robotics as the next AI workload after generative AI.
Global X Robotics & AI ETF (BOTZ) top holdings include Intuitive Surgical, ABB, Keyence, Fanuc, and Nvidia; AUM ~$2.5B with daily volume well above the $5M liquidity floor.

removes.rationale: Quantum stays in the profile but at cap=5 it must yield to a more commercially mature wave; IONQ remains pre-revenue with no near-term inflection, while robotics has named hyperscaler money flowing this quarter.

news_evidence.summary: IONQ reports Q4-2023 revenue of $6.1M and 2024 guide of $37-41M — still pre-commercial at single-digit-million quarterly scale, with widening losses and recent secondary offering diluting holders.
Quantum computing names (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) pulled back 20-40% from late-2023 highs as the cohort's commercial roadmaps slip toward 2026-2027 and AI capex absorbs investor attention.

rationale_overall: At cap=5 we trade quantum (IONQ) for robotics (BOTZ), giving the watchlist one clean ticker per named wave: NVDA (AI), URA (nuclear), RKLB (rockets), LNG (geopolitical-energy), BOTZ (robotics). Figure AI's hyperscaler-backed Series B and Nvidia's GTC GR00T reveal mark the start of robotics' buildup phase, which the profile explicitly names; IONQ's quantum thesis is intact but pre-commercial and can be re-added at a wider cap later.
